solution: Summing \( k\left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{k} \) term by term from \( k=1 \) to \( k=[[N]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the final term \( [[N]]\left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{[[N]]} \), stopping one term early.
This sums \( \left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{k} \) (a plain geometric series), dropping the factor of \( k \).
This adds one extra ratio \( \frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \) to the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.

stem: Evaluate the arithmetic–geometric sum \( \displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{[[N]]} ([[a]] + [[b]]k)\left(\dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{k} \), where \( [[a]]+[[b]]k \) is an arithmetic sequence in \( k \).
solution: Split the sum into \( [[a]]\sum_{k=1}^{[[N]]} r^{k} \) plus \( [[b]]\sum_{k=1}^{[[N]]} k r^{k} \) with \( r=\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \), and add the two closed forms to get \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the final term \( ([[a]]+[[b]]\cdot[[N]])\left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{[[N]]} \), stopping one term early.
This uses only the constant part \( [[a]] \) of the coefficient, as an infinite geometric series, dropping the \( [[b]]k \) part and the finite cutoff.
This adds the constant coefficient \( [[a]] \) once more instead of correctly weighting each term.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.

stem: Evaluate the sum \( \displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{[[N]]} k^{2}\left(\dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{k} \), which combines a geometric factor with a quadratic weight in \( k \).
solution: Summing \( k^{2}\left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{k} \) term by term from \( k=1 \) to \( k=[[N]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the final term \( [[N]]^{2}\left(\frac{[[p]]}{[[q]]}\right)^{[[N]]} \), stopping one term early.
This is the plain (unweighted) infinite geometric sum, dropping the \( k^{2} \) weight entirely.
This adds \( [[N]] \) instead of the correct final term.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
